key_insight: LLMs can help build a MaxSAT solver directly from research papers, but the resulting system still needs careful human guidance and validation.
engineering_relevance: This suggests a practical workflow for rapidly prototyping optimization solvers from literature when starting from scratch rather than an existing codebase.
method: Iterative paper discussion with ChatGPT to derive algorithm design
Codex-based implementation of MaxSAT components and solver routines
LLM-assisted code audits, revisions, and configuration testing
evidence_signal: In fuzzing and MaxSAT Evaluation tests, the reported configurations produced no wrong answers, though performance lagged behind top hand-engineered solvers.
watch_item: The approach remains sensitive to benchmarking quality and human oversight, and solver performance is still below the best specialized implementations.
paper_title: Can LLMs Build a MaxSAT Solver from Papers? The CoreForge Experience
